name: ACT Annual Conference (Association of Corporate Treasurers)
themes: The UK's largest treasury-focused conference and exhibition, the 2026 edition centers on resilience—helping treasury leaders adapt and stay ahead in a changing world. name: eTail Palm Springs 2026 (eTail West)
themes: Leading ecommerce and omnichannel retail conference (WBR) at JW Marriott Desert Springs, focused on digital marketing, customer experience, data/analytics, merchandising, and DTC growth. name: Future Cross Border Payments Summit (FXB Payment Summit)
themes: In-person regional summit (4th edition) on cross-border payments and remittances, covering compliance/AML, instant payments, digital wallets, open banking, and fraud prevention. Attendees are central banks/regulators, banks, fintechs/PSPs, money transfer operators and exchange houses, infrastructure providers, and investors.

name: Future Travel Experience Global (FTE)
themes: FTE Global, billed as "the CES for aviation," gathers 2,000+ senior executives from airlines, airports, vendors, startups and government agencies to explore AI, biometrics, robotics, customer experience, terminal design, baggage tech and sustainability. Audience is overwhelmingly aviation operations, technology and passenger-experience leaders. The 2026 edition marks the event's 20th anniversary.

name: HEDNA Global Distribution Conference (Europe / Lisbon 2026)

Audience skews toward distribution, e-commerce, and revenue/technology decision-makers from hotel brands and partners.

name: ITB Asia

Note: Most of the presentations are in Hungarian, English language is secondary.

agenda_summary: DC Fintech Week 2025 is a four-day fintech week held across multiple venues in Washington, D.C., featuring keynote panels, networking, regulatory discussions and innovation showcases from October 14–17, 2025. Key themes include regulatory stability, adaptation, trust, blockchain, Web 3, and the 'Fintech of the Year' award.

name: EcommScaleUp

Key themes include innovation, AI-driven transformation, AML/fraud/finance regulation, digital lending & corporate, payments and platforms. Brings together senior executives from banks, fintechs, platforms to explore future of digital finance in Europe.

name: EWPN Annual Conference

agenda_summary: The European Women Payments Network (EWPN) Annual Conference 2025 will be held in Amsterdam from October 13–14. The event begins with an Awards Dinner on the evening of October 13, recognizing individuals and organizations promoting diversity and inclusion in the financial industry. The main conference on October 14 will feature keynote discussions, panels, and presentations focusing on financial education and inclusion. EWPN aims to foster a community committed to impactful change in the fintech and payments sectors.

name: FinTech Connect

agenda_summary: FinTech Connect is Europe's only dedicated fintech event for the entire ecosystem, returning this December to the ExCeL exhibition centre in London. Over the course of two days, more than 5000 attendees will meet and network with industry leaders and innovators from across the fintech sector. More than 100 speakers will take to the stage on a range of topics that are expected to define the course of the fintech ecosystem, including the role of AI in financial services and the innovation vs regulation debate. Co-lacted with TOKENIZE:LDN event - The future of tokenization and RWAs

name: Fintech NerdCon

It's designed to bring together ~1,000 senior fintech practitioners in an intimate, content-rich environment without sales booths or expo floors. Organized by Fintech NerdCon: Simon Taylor, Colton Pond, Joy Schwartz.

name: FinTech Week London
themes: ["fintech innovation", "payments", "digital banking", "regulatory compliance", "financial technology", "cross-border payments"]
agenda_summary: Fintech Week London is a week-long series of events that highlight and celebrate London's innovative fintech scene, including a conference which will bring together 1000+ senior decision makers from leading banks, fintechs, investment firms, regulatory bodies, media companies and service providers. Traditional financial institutions come together with new fintechs in one of the world’s oldest financial districts, London. From high-street banks to challengers, technology giants to disruptors, this event offers the opportunity to understand the global themes of fintech, to engage with some of London’s key influencers, decision makers and innovators. This event spans some of the most current themes in fintech and answers those burning questions in a fast-paced and dynamic way – with keynote presentations and interactive panel debates, workshops, roundtables and plenty of networking.

name: FINTECHSHOW
themes: ["Finance Innovation Future", "ai in banking", "cross-border payments", "digital customer experience", "fintech innovation", "fintech regulation", "payment solutions"]

Over two days, it will showcase cutting‑edge Hungarian and international fintech startups, international expert keynotes, panel discussions on AI‑native banking, digital customer experience, fintech trends and regulation, plus networking, awards for top fintech solutions, and an exhibition gallery with 80+ solutions. Note: Most of the presentations are in Hungarian, English language is secondary.

name: Finticipate
themes: ["AI", "AI and digital ID providers", "FinTech", "banks", "blockchain", "consulting firms", "cross-border payments", "crypto and Web3 players", "digital payments", "financial inclusion", "financial innovation", "fintech", "fintech companies and startups", "investors", "payment platforms", "payments", "regulators", "regulatory challenges", "regulatory compliance", "southeastern europe finance", "technology experts"]
agenda_summary: Finticipate – SEE Fintech Forum. A unique fintech event focused on South East Europe, serving as a hub for discussion and idea exchange among fintech professionals. Finticipate is a meeting place for fintech and finance professionals, regulators, and innovators to connect, share insights, and address key trends and challenges shaping the industry in Central and Southeastern Europe and beyond – all with a focus on meaningful content and impactful discussions! And fun! Over the last three editions, forum welcomed over 500 attendees. The audience comprised participants from Albania, Austria, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatia, Cyprus, France, Hungary, Slovenia, Serbia, Malta, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Switzerland, Sweden, Poland, Romania, Bulgaria, the United Arab Emirates, the United Kingdom, and other countries. Previous editions of the Forum have attracted representatives from a diverse range of companies, including traditional banks, payment platforms, neobanks, crypto exchanges, Web3 service providers, startups, fintech payment platforms, international fintech associations, financial institutions, BNPL (Buy Now, Pay Later) services, and many more. Languages of the forum: The simultaneous translation is provided throughout the event (English/Serbian).

The event features visionary keynotes, tailored session journeys, an immersive Experience Center with over a hundred technology solutions, and numerous networking opportunities. Attendees will gain insights into innovation, technology enablers, and build meaningful connections. Key speakers include Mark Cuban and Fiserv CEO Michael Lyons. The 2025 theme emphasizes "Power Moves" for businesses. Registration opens in March 2026 - Make sure to save the date!

The programme includes three content stages, side-events, expos, startup and scaleup tracks, investor activities, and extensive networking. Getting to the event: Copenhagen Airport is conveniently situated in Kastrup, just 6km away from the venue and 8km away from the city centre. Getting to the venue from the airport is a breeze! Just hop on the M2 line of the Metro via Vanløse or catch a cab for a quick ride. If you’d like to take a taxi, you can reach the city centre in around 20-30 minutes. Prices typically range from 250-350 DKK, depending on the traffic. Copenhagen is also easily reachable by ferry from other Nordic countries, including Norway and Sweden. \- By Bus Bus 37 “Raffinaderivej” – 5 min walk from the venue Bus 31 “Holmbladsgade” – 10 min walk from the venue To find more information about public transport by bus navigate to https://www.rejseplanen.dk \- By Metro M2 line (towards Copenhagen Airport). Get off at Lergravsparken St. or Øresund St. – 10 min walk from the venue To find more information about public transportation by metro navigate to https://www.rejseplanen.dk

name: Unchain Festival
themes: ["AI", "AI as an internal efficiency engine rather than a customer-facing tool", "AI vs. Legacy", "AI vs. legacy infrastructure", "EU Inc. Vision", "EU cohesion and regional resilience", "EU cohesion/resilience", "EU internal cohesion and regional resilience", "Embedded Everything", "automation limits / infrastructure risk in modern banking", "cross-industry finance", "embedded finance", "embedded finance/payments", "embedded financial models beyond checkout", "execution", "execution gaps in European banking", "finance integration", "financial ecosystem", "fintech", "hybrid digital\u2013human banking experiences", "legacy infrastructure", "legacy infrastructure / legacy systems", "partnerships", "payments", "programmable capital markets", "strategic alignment"]
agenda_summary: Unchain Festival Unchain unites the Central and Eastern Europe as the growth market of Europe and a Global center of tech development. It provides a nexus for the financial industry and financial technology providers to come together. This year’s UNCHAIN Festival is positioned less as a “fintech event” and more as a meeting point for the entire financial ecosystem, where execution—not ideas—is the central theme. It brings together senior decision-makers (often C-level) from banking, fintech, payments, insurance, retail, and technology, alongside regulators, central banks, and EU institutions, reflecting how finance is now deeply cross-industry. The core message is that European finance has moved beyond innovation hype into a phase where legacy systems, AI adoption, and infrastructure decisions are make-or-break issues. Because finance is becoming embedded across sectors, the audience is no longer just banks—merchants, tech providers, and even non-financial companies entering financial services are increasingly relevant participants. The event is designed for people who need strategic alignment, partnerships, and practical execution insights, rather than early-stage inspiration or purely technical deep dives. In practical terms: if you’re a decision-maker shaping products, infrastructure, regulation, or partnerships in finance (or finance-adjacent industries), this event is for you; if you’re looking for hands-on coding, early startup pitching, or niche tech discussions, it’s likely not. Overall, UNCHAIN 2026 is best understood as a high-level, cross-industry strategy forum for those actively building or reshaping financial ecosystems in Europe. English is the official language of the event, ensuring clear communication for diverse Central and Eastern European audience.
